name: Security Savings Bank
description_short: Community bank serving Monmouth and Stronghurst, Illinois with checking, savings, CDs, auto loans, and mortgages via online and in-person banking.
description_long:
Security Savings Bank is a community-focused financial institution operating in Warren County, Illinois with two physical locations in Monmouth and Stronghurst. The bank has been positioned as a local alternative to larger national banks, emphasizing customer relationships and community involvement as core to their mission. Founded to serve the everyday banking needs of residents in rural Illinois, Security Savings Bank maintains a traditional branch banking model with extended hours and personalized service. The institution operates under routing number 271172644 and provides both personal and business banking services.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of deposit and lending products. Deposit accounts include statement savings (up to 0.25% APY), RoundUp Savings (up to 0.70% APY), and CDs (up to 3.30% APY). Loan products include auto financing (currently advertising rates as low as 4.36% APR), mortgages for home purchase and refinance, personal loans, and business banking options. All customers have access to online banking with mobile app support (App Store and Google Play available) and a network of ATMs.

Security Savings Bank differentiates itself through its hyperlocal presence and community involvement. Rather than operating as a large regional or national chain, the bank maintains exactly two branch locations with specific lobby and drive-up hours tailored to local needs. The Monmouth location offers extended Friday hours (until 5pm) and Saturday service, while both branches maintain limited Saturday hours. The bank provides free financial education resources and calculators to help customers with budgeting, loan payments, retirement planning, and other financial decisions.

As a small community bank, Security Savings Bank is best suited for customers prioritizing personal relationships and local decision-making over competitive national rates. However, prospective customers should note that deposit rates (0.25-0.70% APY) are significantly below national online bank averages, and the bank's limited geographic footprint restricts access to only two physical locations. The institution does not appear to offer specialized products like credit cards, business lines of credit, or investment services, limiting its appeal for customers seeking comprehensive financial services from a single provider. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Certificate of Deposit (CD) accounts (up to 3.30% APY)
Mortgage lending for home purchase and refinance
Online banking platform with bill pay
Financial calculators (loan payment, budgeting, retirement planning)
pros: Two convenient branch locations in Monmouth and Stronghurst with extended hours (Monmouth open Friday until 5pm, Saturday mornings)
Competitive auto loan rates currently advertised at 4.36% APR during spring promotion
CD rates up to 3.30% APY, above many traditional brick-and-mortar banks
Full online and mobile banking access with apps available on App Store and Google Play
Free financial education resources, calculators, and financial planning tools
Dedicated customer service team and personalized service through small-town banking model
Business banking enrollment available for small business owners
cons: Deposit rates extremely low: statement savings at 0.25% APY and RoundUp Savings at 0.70% APY are well below national online bank averages (3-5% APY)
Limited geographic footprint restricted to two Illinois locations (Monmouth and Stronghurst) with no ATM expansion outside service area mentioned
Minimal product offerings compared to full-service banks—no credit cards, investment services, or business lines of credit visible on website
Stronghurst branch has reduced lobby hours (8:30am-3:30pm weekdays) limiting accessibility for working customers
Small institution may lack resources and technology infrastructure of larger banks for complex financial services
best_for: Rural Illinois residents in Warren County seeking personalized banking with local decision-making
Auto buyers taking advantage of seasonal promotional rates (currently 4.36% APR)
Homebuyers and refinancers preferring relationship-based mortgage lending in small-town settings
Customers prioritizing in-person service and community involvement over maximum deposit rates
diagnosis: Security Savings Bank is best for small-town Illinois customers who prioritize personal relationships and local service over competitive rates and product breadth. The primary caveat is that this institution is fundamentally limited by geography (two locations only) and offers significantly below-market deposit rates, making it unsuitable for rate-sensitive savers or customers outside Warren County seeking convenient access.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
